channel.title: Code Defence Intelligence Brief — Wed, 25 Mar 2026
channel.description: Daily cybersecurity intelligence for Wed, 25 Mar 2026.

item.title: TeamPCP poisons LiteLLM AI package to steal cloud and SSH secrets. 🐛
item.description:
Your AI development tools are being weaponized to harvest your most sensitive infrastructure keys. 🐛
Supply Chain Alert · TeamPCP backdoors LiteLLM PyPI package versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8.
In the last 24 hours‚ researchers identified that the LiteLLM Python library was compromised after TeamPCP gained access to maintainer credentials via earlier attacks on Trivy and Checkmarx @[GitHub](urn:li:organization:19041) Actions. The malicious versions ❨1.82.7 and 1.82.8❩ contain a high-capability credential harvester that targets SSH keys‚ AWS/Azure/GCP tokens‚ and Kubernetes secrets.
The attack is particularly dangerous because version 1.82.8 utilizes a .pth file hijack. This ensures the malware executes every time any Python script is run in the environment‚ even if LiteLLM is never imported. This represents a significant escalation in TeamPCP industrialization of supply chain vulnerabilities.
The uncomfortable truth: Your AI innovation speed is creating blind spots in your dependency management that attackers are now exploiting to gain persistent root access to your cloud clusters.
→ Immediately uninstall LiteLLM versions 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 and revert to the last safe release ❨1.82.6❩. → Rotate all SSH keys‚ cloud provider secrets‚ and CI/CD tokens that were present on any system where the malicious versions were installed. → Implement pin-to-hash policies for all PyPI and GitHub dependencies to prevent automated tag-based poisoning.
#Cybersecurity #SupplyChain #AISecurity #DevSecOps #AppSec #CodeDefence

item.title: Oracle issues emergency fix for critical Identity Manager RCE flaw. 🛡️
item.description:
Your core identity infrastructure is open to unauthenticated remote takeover. 🛡️
CVE-2026-21992 · Severity 9.8 · Unauthenticated RCE in Oracle Identity Manager.
@[Oracle](urn:li:organization:1009) has released an out-of-band security update to address a critical flaw in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager. This vulnerability can be exploited over a network without the need for a username or password. If successfully exploited‚ an attacker can execute arbitrary code and gain full administrative control over the identity environment.
Because Identity Manager is the heart of user provisioning and access management for many enterprises‚ a compromise here is a total loss scenario for corporate security. Attackers are already scanning for vulnerable instances following the disclosure.
The uncomfortable truth: Your centralized identity system is your greatest security asset‚ but it is also the single most attractive target for an adversary looking to bypass every other control in your stack.
→ Apply the Oracle March 2026 emergency security updates for Identity Manager and Web Services Manager immediately. → Strictly restrict network access to identity management consoles to internal administrative segments only. → Audit your identity logs for unauthorized administrative actions or anomalous service account creations.
#Cybersecurity #IdentitySecurity #Oracle #PatchManagement #CISO #CodeDefence

item.title: Citrix urges immediate patching of critical NetScaler data leak flaw. 🛑
item.description:
Your gateway is leaking sensitive session data to unauthenticated attackers. 🛑
CVE-2026-3055 · Severity 9.3 · Critical Memory Overread in NetScaler ADC & Gateway.
@[Citrix](urn:li:organization:1598) has released emergency security updates for a critical vulnerability in NetScaler ADC and Gateway. The flaw allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to perform a memory overread and leak sensitive information from the application process memory.
Leaked data can include session tokens and administrative credentials. This is a maximum-priority risk for organizations using Citrix for remote access‚ as session hijacking can bypass multi-factor authentication entirely.
The uncomfortable truth: Your perimeter security tools are the most attractive targets for attackers because they often possess the highest level of trust and access to your internal network.
→ Update NetScaler ADC and Gateway to the latest patched releases immediately. → Force a global password reset for all administrative accounts that have logged into the Gateway in the last 48 hours. → Monitor for unusual session activity originating from non-standard or geographic IP blocks.
#Cybersecurity #NetworkSecurity #Citrix #PatchManagement #SOC #CodeDefence

item.title: Final remediation deadline for critical n8n RCE vulnerability today. 🛡️
item.description:
The window for patching a critical automation platform flaw under active exploitation closes today. 🛡️
CVE-2025-68613 · Severity 10.0 · CISA KEV Remediation Deadline: March 25‚ 2026.
The @[CISA](urn:li:organization:13010360) has mandated that all federal agencies remediate a critical RCE flaw in the n8n automation platform by the end of today. This vulnerability allows an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code with the same privileges as the n8n process.
Automation platforms are high-value targets because they often possess deep administrative access to connected SaaS apps and databases. Attackers are currently leveraging this flaw to establish persistence and move laterally across enterprise networks.
The uncomfortable truth: If your automation platform is unpatched and reachable today‚ you are effectively granting root access to your entire business logic to an adversary.
→ Update n8n to version 1.120.4‚ 1.121.1‚ or 1.122.0 immediately. → Restrict workflow creation and editing to fully trusted users only. → Run n8n in a hardened environment with restricted operating system privileges.
#Cybersecurity #AppSec #Automation #ZeroTrust #SOC #CodeDefence
